claim: The Arade River is effectively dry or reduced to isolated pools in its middle and upper reaches during summer — average base flow drops below 1 m³/s — making it a non-barrier and potential water-attractor for lost dogs only in autumn through spring.
stance_target: seasonal water source modeling for lost dog search prioritization
scope_conditions: Arade River above estuary; summer season (June-September)
quoted_support: Summer base flows are often less than 1 m³/s, exacerbated by upstream dams that reduce natural flow, contributing to sediment silting in the estuary.
numbers: winter peak flows up to 200 m³/s
average annual discharge 1.5-3 m³/s at mouth

claim: The Odelouca Dam (2009, 134 hm³ capacity) supplies public water to over 1 million inhabitants in western Algarve and forms a 7 km² reservoir on the Odelouca tributary — a permanent large water body accessible to wildlife and dogs in Serra de Monchique foothills.
stance_target: Barragem de Odelouca as permanent water attractor in rescue search area
scope_conditions: Odelouca tributary, Algarve Barlavento; operational since 2009
quoted_support: The Odelouca Dam (Barragem de Odelouca)... has a height of 76 meters above natural ground, a crest length of 418 meters, and a useful storage capacity of 134 million cubic meters, forming a reservoir with a surface area of about 7 km². Primarily intended for regional public water supply to over one million inhabitants in western Algarve municipalities.

claim: The Arade River has flooded the Portimão area in 2003, 2004, 2007, 2010, and 2015 — winter flooding events make lower river zones temporarily impassable barriers for dogs.
stance_target: flood hazard zones in lower Arade basin for seasonal rescue routing
scope_conditions: Lower Arade basin around Portimão; winter high-flow events
quoted_support: Peak flows during winter rains can reach up to 200 m³/s or more, leading to occasional flooding in the lower basin, as evidenced by historical events in 2003, 2004, 2007, 2010, and 2015 that affected the Portimão area.

claim: The Arade Dam (1956) supports irrigation of ~2,300 ha of farmland in Silves, Lagoa, and Portimão; agricultural runoff from these zones causes nitrate concentrations up to ~200 µM — elevated above EU limits — affecting water quality downstream.
stance_target: Arade dam irrigation zone as agricultural human activity layer in territorial map
scope_conditions: Arade River basin lower section; current conditions
quoted_support: Water quality is generally good in upstream sections but deteriorates downstream due to agricultural runoff, with moderate nutrient levels including nitrate concentrations up to ~200 µM.

claim: The Arade estuary (15 km²) is designated as Natura 2000 site PTCON0008, hosting >100 avifauna species and endemic Arade chub (Squalius aradensis) — a sensitive ecological zone where search operations should minimize disturbance.
stance_target: Arade estuary ecological sensitivity for rescue coordination in coastal Portimão area
scope_conditions: Arade River estuary near Portimão/Ferragudo; EU Natura 2000 designation 2008
quoted_support: Conservation efforts for the Arade River include its designation in 2008 as part of the European Natura 2000 network (site PTCON0008), which safeguards approximately 15 km² of associated wetlands and estuarine habitats critical for bird and fish species.

claim: The Ribeira de Odelouca (92 km, originating in Serra de Monchique) contributes over half the Arade basin area through its 507 km² sub-basin and retains flow longer than the main Arade stem due to higher elevation headwaters.
stance_target: Odelouca tributary as primary water corridor in Serra de Monchique foothills for search routing
scope_conditions: Odelouca stream, Serra de Monchique to Arade confluence; hydrological data
quoted_support: The river's primary tributary is the Ribeira de Odelouca, the longest at 92 km, which rises in the Serra de Monchique and contributes over half of the basin's area through its own 507 km² sub-basin.
numbers: endemic cyprinids up to 90% of abundance in tributaries
